title: Within-driver pairwise ranker for AUC
summary: ROC AUC is a pairwise ranking metric, and each driver-race is a sparse event timeline with many easy negatives.
rationale: ROC AUC is a pairwise ranking metric, and each driver-race is a sparse event timeline with many easy negatives. A pointwise classifier can waste capacity on probability calibration instead of ordering the few laps that matter near the stop window.
implementation_hint: Train a LightGBM ranker with groups=`Race_Year x Driver` using the current feature set; compare `lambdarank` vs `rank_xendcg`, then blend its percentile rank with the existing classifier output rather than averaging raw probabilities.
expected_effect: Improves ordering of candidate pit laps inside each stint/race sequence, which should help local and public AUC even if calibration does not improve.
risk: Some groups have very few or zero positive laps, so grouping and negative sampling need care; raw ranker scores may transfer poorly without rank-based blending.
